comments.body: Claiming a narrow #119 docs-routing brick from current `fork/main`. Scope: add the Merkle probabilistic binding / simulation-based hiding residual surface to `docs/kb/audits/ARKLIB-PROOF-GAP-INDEX-2026-06-06.md`, pointing at the existing deterministic core in `MerkleTree/Extraction.lean` and the salted hiding definition in `MerkleTree/Hiding.lean`. This will not claim the RO collision bound or hiding hybrid proof is formalized.

Pushed a narrow #119 docs-routing brick to `main`.

Commit: `c3f47210e docs(#119): route Merkle security gap`

What changed:
- Added the Merkle probabilistic RO-collision binding / simulation-based salted hiding surface to `docs/kb/audits/ARKLIB-PROOF-GAP-INDEX-2026-06-06.md`:
  - `Merkle probabilistic RO-collision binding and simulation-based salted hiding` -> #119

Validation:
- `git diff --check -- docs/kb/audits/ARKLIB-PROOF-GAP-INDEX-2026-06-06.md`
- anchor search confirms the #119 row is present beside the existing commitment placeholder routing.

Leaving #119 open: this only fixes central ownership/routing. It does not prove the VCVio RO-collision probability bound or the Merkle hiding simulator/hybrid argument.

Claiming a narrow #119 audit follow-up from current fork/main. Scope: add focused #print axioms coverage for the already-proved Merkle deterministic binding/extraction front doors in Extraction.lean and the salted construction/hiding-definition front doors in Hiding.lean. This is audit/regression coverage only; it will not claim the RO collision probability bound or simulation-based hiding proof is formalized.

Pushed a narrow #119 Merkle audit follow-up to main in autosync commit `9c455a697`.

What changed in the Merkle files:
- `ArkLib/CommitmentScheme/MerkleTree/Extraction.lean`: added `#print axioms` coverage for the deterministic binding/extraction front doors:
  - `getPutativeRootWithHash_injective_of_hash_injective`
  - `opening_binding`
  - `extractLeaf_eq_opened`
  - `extracted_leaf_unique`
  - `multi_instance_extracted_leaves_unique`
- `ArkLib/CommitmentScheme/MerkleTree/Hiding.lean`: added `#print axioms` coverage for the salted construction / hiding-definition front doors:
  - `saltedLeaves_get`
  - `salted_completeness`
  - `openTranscript`
  - `Hiding`

Validation:
- `git diff --check 9c455a697^ 9c455a697 -- ArkLib/CommitmentScheme/MerkleTree/Extraction.lean ArkLib/CommitmentScheme/MerkleTree/Hiding.lean` passed.
- `python3 scripts/forbidden_tokens.py ArkLib/CommitmentScheme/MerkleTree/Extraction.lean ArkLib/CommitmentScheme/MerkleTree/Hiding.lean` passed (it also emitted the existing stale residual-axiom allow-list warning for `fenziSanso_upperBound_attack_concrete_residual`).
- `python3 scripts/sorry_census.py --root .` reports `holes: 0`.
- `git show --check --oneline 9c455a697 -- ArkLib/CommitmentScheme/MerkleTree/Extraction.lean ArkLib/CommitmentScheme/MerkleTree/Hiding.lean` passed.

Focused `lake env lean ArkLib/CommitmentScheme/MerkleTree/Extraction.lean` was attempted. After hydrating `Mathlib.Analysis.Convex.Topology`, it still stopped before reaching Merkle source on missing `Mathlib/Analysis/Normed/Module/Ball/Pointwise.olean`, with the shared checkout also warning that the CompPoly manifest is out of date.

Leaving #119 open: this is audit coverage only. It does not prove the RO collision probability bound or the simulation-based salted hiding/hybrid argument.
Claiming issue 119. I am working on proving probabilistic RO-collision binding and simulation-based hiding for Merkle commitments.

Claiming a narrow disjoint #119 proof brick from current `main`. Scope: add deterministic same-salt salted-opening binding lemmas in `ArkLib/CommitmentScheme/MerkleTree/Hiding.lean`, reusing the collision-free core from `Extraction.lean`. This will not touch `CapacityBounds`, `BatchedFri`, or scratch files, and it will not claim the probabilistic RO-collision bound or simulation-based hiding hybrid is formalized.

Pushed a narrow deterministic #119 proof brick to `main`.

Commit: `1fef427b3 feat(#119): add salted Merkle same-salt binding`

What changed:
- `ArkLib/CommitmentScheme/MerkleTree/Hiding.lean` now imports the deterministic extraction core and proves `salted_opening_binding_value`: under the same collision-free compression assumption used by `Extraction.lean`, two salted openings at the same index with the same salt, sibling path, and root cannot reveal different underlying leaf values.
- Added the new theorem to the local `#print axioms` audit list.

Validation:
- `git diff --check 1fef427b3^ 1fef427b3 -- ArkLib/CommitmentScheme/MerkleTree/Hiding.lean` passed.
- `python3 scripts/forbidden_tokens.py ArkLib/CommitmentScheme/MerkleTree/Hiding.lean` passed; it only emitted the existing stale residual-axiom allow-list warning.
- Focused Lean checks are blocked in this checkout: direct `lake env lean ArkLib/CommitmentScheme/MerkleTree/Extraction.lean` stops before Merkle source because `VCVio.olean` is missing, and `lake build ArkLib.CommitmentScheme.MerkleTree.Extraction` hits stale/incompatible dependency object files. A clean temp worktree direct `lean` check is not the right check for the new local import because it needs prebuilt ArkLib `.olean`s.

Remaining blocker for the full issue:
- Probabilistic binding still needs a formal VCVio/random-oracle collision-probability event/bound that turns the deterministic no-collision implication into negligible adversarial binding failure.
- Simulation-based hiding still needs a formal Merkle simulator/transcript distribution plus a hybrid/uniformity lemma for unopened salted leaves under the salt sampler / random oracle. This brick does not discharge either probabilistic object.

Claiming a narrow deterministic #119 proof brick from current `main` (`438501479`). Scope: extend `ArkLib/CommitmentScheme/MerkleTree/Hiding.lean` with a pair-level salted opening binding lemma: under the existing collision-free compression assumption, two salted openings at the same index with the same sibling path and root determine both the salt and the underlying value (`salt₁ = salt₂ ∧ value₁ = value₂`). This builds on the deterministic `opening_binding` core and complements the same-salt value-only lemma; it does not claim any random-oracle collision-probability bound or simulation-based hiding hybrid.

Pushed a narrow deterministic #119 proof brick to `main`.

Commit: `5a5bf8847` (`feat(#119): prove salted Merkle pair binding`)

What changed in `ArkLib/CommitmentScheme/MerkleTree/Hiding.lean`:
- Added `InductiveMerkleTree.salted_opening_binding_pair`.
- Under the existing collision-free compression hypothesis, two salted openings at the same index with the same sibling path and root determine the full salted opening pair:
  - `salt₁ = salt₂ ∧ value₁ = value₂`.
- This complements `salted_opening_binding_value`, which handles the same-salt value-only form.
- Added the theorem to the local `#print axioms` audit block.

Validation:
- `lean ArkLib/CommitmentScheme/MerkleTree/Hiding.lean` using the hydrated `LEAN_PATH` passed.
- `#print axioms InductiveMerkleTree.salted_opening_binding_pair` reports no axioms.
- `git diff --check -- ArkLib/CommitmentScheme/MerkleTree/Hiding.lean` passed before commit.
- `python3 scripts/forbidden_tokens.py ArkLib/CommitmentScheme/MerkleTree/Hiding.lean` passed.
- `python3 scripts/sorry_census.py --root .` reports `holes=0`.
- Remote `main` contains `5a5bf8847`.

Leaving #119 open: this strengthens the deterministic no-collision salted-binding core only. The probabilistic random-oracle collision bound and simulation-based hiding hybrid are still the real remaining work.

Claiming one follow-up deterministic #119 brick on current `main` (`5a5bf8847`). Scope: use `salted_opening_binding_pair` plus `salted_completeness` to prove an honest-tree extractor uniqueness lemma: if a candidate `(salt, value)` verifies against the honest salted root using the honest authentication path at `idx`, then `salt = salts.get idx ∧ value = leaves.get idx`. This remains a deterministic no-collision statement only; it does not prove the RO collision-probability bound or hiding simulator/hybrid.

Pushed the follow-up deterministic #119 brick to `main`.

Commit: `da6f67820` (`feat(#119): prove honest salted opening uniqueness`)

What changed in `ArkLib/CommitmentScheme/MerkleTree/Hiding.lean`:
- Added `InductiveMerkleTree.salted_opening_unique_against_honest_tree`.
- If a candidate salted pair `(salt, value)` verifies against the honest salted root using the honest authentication path at `idx`, then collision-free compression forces:
  - `salt = salts.get idx ∧ value = leaves.get idx`.
- The proof composes `salted_opening_binding_pair` with `salted_completeness` / `saltedLeaves_get`.
- Added a `#print axioms` audit line.

Validation on the rebased pushed tip:
- `lean ArkLib/CommitmentScheme/MerkleTree/Hiding.lean` using the hydrated `LEAN_PATH` passed.
- New axiom audit reports `[propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound]` through `salted_completeness`; no `sorryAx`.
- `git diff --check -- ArkLib/CommitmentScheme/MerkleTree/Hiding.lean` passed before commit.
- `python3 scripts/forbidden_tokens.py ArkLib/CommitmentScheme/MerkleTree/Hiding.lean` passed.
- `python3 scripts/sorry_census.py --root .` reports `holes=0`.
- Remote `main` contains `da6f67820`.

Leaving #119 open: this is deterministic extractor/binding plumbing for honest salted openings. The random-oracle collision-probability theorem and simulation-based hiding proof remain open.
